findings.evidence: The only Hopf-operation lemmas in this file are functoriality lemmas at lines 141-152; Mathlib supplies the underlying tensor lemmas `TensorProduct.comul_tmul`/`counit_tmul` in `Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/TensorProduct.lean:63-70` and `TensorProduct.antipode_def` in `Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/TensorProduct.lean:72-74`, but there are no `BaseChange` handles for them.
findings.fix: Add the characteristic simp lemmas for `Coalgebra.counit`, `Coalgebra.comul`, and `HopfAlgebra.antipode` on `r ⊗ₜ[k] a`, plus the corresponding `includeRight` forms where usable, so consumers need not unfold `Obj` or use tensor-product internals directly.
findings.issue: `Obj` is introduced as the Hopf base-change object, but the file provides no `BaseChange` restatements for the Hopf operations on `K ⊗[k] A` (`counit`, `comul`, `antipode`) on tensors or on `includeRight`.

findings.evidence: Lines 41-42 define `includeRight` by reducible alias to `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight`; lines 80-81 define `map` by reducible alias to `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map (BialgHom.id K K) f`, and several public lemmas are proved by `rfl` from those bodies.
findings.fix: Make these opaque `def`s and keep the intended behavior available through the existing pointwise/functorial simp lemmas, adding any missing characteristic lemmas instead of relying on reducibility.
findings.issue: `includeRight` and `map` are public `abbrev`s, so their bodies are part of the usable surface and downstream proofs can reduce them to `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight` and `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map`.

summary: The base-change name is useful, but the public API does not yet characterize the Hopf structure it names and exposes reducible aliases for the bundled maps. Downstream code would either unfold through Mathlib internals or depend on generic map lemmas instead of stable TauCeti handles.
